name: Tokyo
notes:
**Best time for a budget‑friendly student trip** | Season | Why it’s great | Key places to hit | Tips | |--------|----------------|-------------------|------| | **Late March – Early April (Golden Week ends)** | Mild weather, cherry blossoms, lower flight prices after the peak tourist rush. | • Kyoto’s Maruyama Park, Arashiyama
• Tokyo’s Ueno Park
• Nara’s Todaiji Temple | Book flights 2–3 months ahead. Use local bus passes. | | **Late May – Early June (Early Hakutaka season)** | Warm, clear skies, fewer tourists than summer. | • Osaka’s Dotonbori
• Kanazawa’s Kenrokuen Garden
• Hokkaido’s Furano (before heavy rain) | Stay in hostels or capsule hotels. | | **Late September – Early October (Autumn foliage)** | Stunning fall colors, temperate temps, post‑summer price drop. | • Nikko’s Toshogu Shrine
• Nikko National Park (leaf peeping)
• Hirosaki Castle in Aomori | Rent a JR Pass after 2 days in Tokyo; use it for long‑distance trains. | | **Late November – Early December (Pre‑New Year)** | Cool but comfortable, cheaper accommodations, fewer crowds. | • Kyoto’s Kiyomizu‑dera at dusk
• Tokyo’s Tsukiji Outer Market (closed 3‑4 pm)
• Hakone’s Owakudani | Buy a Japan Rail Pass (7‑day) – it pays off if you travel between cities. | **General budget hacks** 1. **Flights** – Fly into Narita or Haneda from nearby hubs (e.g., Osaka) to snag cheaper fares. 2. **Transport** – Use regional rail passes (e.g., JR East Pass for Kanto, JR West Pass for Kansai) instead of a nationwide pass if you’ll stay within one area. 3. **Food** – Eat at conveyor‑belt sushi (kaiten), 100‑yen bento shops, and local izakayas. 4. **Accommodations** – Book hostels (e.g., K’s House Tokyo, Sakura Hostel) or capsule hotels; stay in neighborhoods like Kichijoji (Tokyo) or Namba (Osaka) for cheap 24‑hour eateries. **Bottom line:** Late March–April or late September–early October give the best mix of weather, sights, and affordability for students. Book early, use regional passes, and stay in budget hostels to keep costs low.
